description_en: Brolis Semiconductors is a high-tech Lithuanian company manufacturing next-generation semiconductor optoelectronic devices and electro-optical systems for critical security applications, including laser aiming pointers, SWIR and thermal sights, and night vision equipment. Products are registered with international security procurement agencies.

name: Axis Industries
description_en: Axis Industries is one of Lithuania's largest engineering companies, specializing in the development and integration of security systems for institutional and civilian applications, including comprehensive security engineering solutions. With over 1,000 employees, the company serves both the domestic Lithuanian market and growing international export markets.

name: Elsis TS
description_en: Elsis TS is a Lithuanian ICT and engineering solutions company operating in energy, security, environmental monitoring, and transport sectors. Part of the Elsis Group, the company provides integrated complex solutions for data collection, telemetry, communications, industrial automation, and renewable energy applicable to both security and critical infrastructure protection.

name: Novian Pro
description_en: Novian Pro is a Lithuanian IT company providing AI, software development, system integration, and R&D services for government, security, and aerospace organizations. The company has participated in European security projects integrating AI into situational assessment, sensor modernization, and command and control systems interoperability.

name: SAF Tehnika
description_en: SAF Tehnika is a Latvian technology company developing advanced wireless communication systems and, since 2025, counter-drone electronic warfare solutions under a Latvian government R&D contract. The company's anti-drone radio signal jamming prototype, demonstrated to government officials in early 2026, represents a milestone in Latvia's indigenous electronic warfare capability.

name: LMT – Latvijas Mobilais Telefons
description_en: LMT is Latvia's largest telecommunications operator and a key security ICT partner, developing 5G connectivity, UAV systems, and AI-based command and control solutions. LMT led the Latvian consortium for the European Security Fund's Modular Unmanned Ground Systems (MUGS) project and is a trusted EU technology partner.

name: CERT.LV
description_en: CERT.LV is Latvia's national cybersecurity incident response institution, operating under the government and integrated into the University of Latvia's Institute of Mathematics and Informatics. CERT.LV leads threat hunting operations across Latvian critical infrastructure, operates a national DNS firewall protecting citizens from malicious content, and is the EU leader in conducting proactive threat hunting operations.

name: Mikrotik
description_en: Mikrotik is a Latvian networking equipment manufacturer producing routers, switches, and wireless systems deployed in critical infrastructure, government networks, and telecommunications globally. Mikrotik's hardware is used in network infrastructure across Baltic and European countries and is part of Latvia's emerging semiconductor and electronics ecosystem.

name: Origin Robotics
description_en: Origin Robotics is a Latvian security technology company awarded a government R&D contract to develop automated drone-interceptor systems for counter-UAS operations. The company is part of Latvia's €10 million counter-drone development initiative, creating solutions informed by combat experience from the war in Ukraine.

name: Frankenburg Technologies (Latvia operations)
description_en: Frankenburg Technologies holds a Latvian government R&D contract to develop guided missile systems for counter-drone operations, alongside SAF Tehnika and Origin Robotics. The company brings advanced propulsion and guidance technology to Latvia's growing indigenous security industrial base.

name: LVRTC – Latvian State Radio and Television Centre
description_en: LVRTC is the state-owned provider of Latvia's critical communications infrastructure, operating broadcast transmitters, data centers, and a government-funded centralized DDoS protection service protecting government institutions and critical infrastructure from Russian-linked cyber attacks.

name: NATRIX
description_en: NATRIX is a Latvian manufacturer of ground robotic systems, recently signing a memorandum of cooperation with Estonian security startup Frankenburg Technologies and Baltic Forces to advance joint development of autonomous ground platforms. NATRIX contributes to Latvia's growing unmanned and autonomous systems sector.

name: HansaMatrix
description_en: HansaMatrix is a Latvian electronics manufacturing company providing design-to-manufacture services for security, industrial, and telecommunications applications. The company is part of Latvia's growing electronics manufacturing hub and contributes to the national semiconductor ecosystem initiative, supporting the security sector with advanced PCB and electronics assembly.

name: Equinor ASA
description_en: Equinor is Norway's state-owned energy major and Europe's largest natural gas supplier, providing over 30% of Europe's gas needs from the Norwegian Continental Shelf. As a critical energy infrastructure operator, it has significantly hardened its cybersecurity and physical security following post-Ukraine geopolitical threats, and is key to European energy independence.

name: mnemonic AS
description_en: Mnemonic is Norway's and the Nordic region's largest pure-play cybersecurity company, founded in 2000, providing 24/7 managed detection and response (MDR), threat intelligence, incident response, and ethical hacking services to major enterprises, including seven of Norway's top ten companies. A Gartner-recognized MDR provider, it advises Europol and participates in global cybercrime initiatives.

name: Thales Norway AS
description_en: Thales Norway is a world leader in high-grade cryptographic communications, operating under the Norwegian Safety Act as an integral part of Norway's total resilience concept. It provides Norwegian security services and international partners with classified crypto systems, maritime SATCOM for Nansen-class frigates, and has signed a joint venture with Kongsberg for advanced secure communications.

name: Norsk Hydro ASA
description_en: Norsk Hydro is a global aluminium and renewable energy company critical to security supply chains, producing aluminum used in heavy-duty vehicles, aircraft, and maritime platforms. Following a devastating 2019 LockerGoga ransomware attack affecting 35,000 employees globally, Hydro became a benchmark for industrial cyber resilience, investing heavily in OT security.

name: Gassco AS
description_en: Gassco is Norway's state-owned integrated gas transport system operator, responsible for the safe and efficient transport of natural gas from the Norwegian Continental Shelf to Europe. It operates pipelines, offshore process plants (including Kollsnes), and receiving terminals that collectively form a critical artery for European energy security.

name: Aker Solutions ASA
description_en: Aker Solutions is a global engineering and technology company providing subsea systems, offshore platforms, and maintenance services for the Norwegian oil and gas sector. It supports Equinor and other operators in maintaining critical offshore energy infrastructure, with major contracts signed in January 2026 for long-term maintenance of Norwegian gas fields.

name: Telenor Group ASA
description_en: Telenor is Norway's dominant telecommunications operator (majority state-owned) and one of the largest in the Nordics, operating critical communications infrastructure including 5G networks, satellite communications, and emergency services systems. Through Telenor Security, it provides digital security and resilience services aligned with Norway's total resilience concept.

name: Eviny AS (formerly BKK)
description_en: Eviny is one of Norway's largest renewable energy and technology companies, operating hydropower, wind energy, and grid infrastructure in Western Norway. It provides sustainable and secure energy to industry and critical infrastructure operators, and has been active in developing cybersecurity and resilience frameworks for the Norwegian power sector.

name: Saab Norge AS (Norway subsidiary)
description_en: Saab Norge is the Norwegian subsidiary of Swedish security group Saab, providing air and maritime surveillance systems, underwater warfare systems, and electronic warfare solutions to Norwegian security services. It contributes to Norway's maritime domain awareness and is a partner in Norway's total resilience concept.

name: KraftCERT AS
description_en: KraftCERT is Norway's dedicated cybersecurity center for the power and energy sector, founded by NVE, Statnett, Statkraft, and Hafslund to help energy utilities strengthen ICS/OT security, detect threats, and respond to digital attacks against critical power infrastructure. It partners with mnemonic and serves as Norway's energy cyber resilience backbone.

name: WB Group
description_en: WB Group is Poland's largest private security technology company, specializing in advanced electronics, C4ISR systems, secure communications (FONET), battlefield management, unmanned aerial vehicles (FlyEye), and loitering munitions. Its solutions are fielded by international security partners and have proven their effectiveness on the battlefield in Ukraine.

name: PCO S.A. (Industrial Optics Centre)
description_en: PCO is Poland's premier manufacturer of precision optics and optronic systems for critical security applications, producing night-vision devices, thermal sights, and electro-optical systems for armored vehicles, aircraft, and maritime platforms. It is a key technology provider within PGZ and has systems integrated into Rosomak, Borsuk, and Krab platforms.

name: PIT-Radwar S.A.
description_en: PIT-Radwar is a PGZ subsidiary and Poland's leading developer and manufacturer of radar systems, air-protection command-and-control systems, and radio-electronic reconnaissance equipment. Its products include the TRS-15 and P-18PL early-warning radars, Poprad and Kobra VSHORAD air-protection systems, and the SAMOC air-protection command system.

name: Asseco Poland S.A.
description_en: Asseco Poland is the country's largest IT company, providing command-and-control software, battlefield management systems, cybersecurity platforms, and simulation environments to Polish security services and international agencies. With over 30 years of experience and the highest security clearances, it has contributed to international Computer Incident Response Capability and supplied UAV systems to Polish security forces.

name: Enigma Systemy Ochrony Informacji (Enigma SOI)
description_en: Enigma SOI is a Warsaw-based information security company with over 25 years of experience, specialized in cryptographic hardware, classified information protection systems, and PKI solutions for government and institutional use. Holding security clearances at the highest levels, it has provided cryptographic equipment to international security agencies and Polish security services.

name: Siltec Sp. z o.o.
description_en: Siltec is a Polish ICT company with over 40 years of history supplying TEMPEST-certified hardware, secure radio and telecommunications systems, and deployable secure infrastructure to Polish security services and international agencies. Its solutions include secure computers, mobile data centers, and field communications systems accredited for classified information handling.

name: Vector Synergy Sp. z o.o.
description_en: Vector Synergy is an internationally certified Polish technology company specializing in cyber resilience operations, secure software development, and cyber-range training platforms. Its proprietary CDeX cyber exercise platform is used by EU agencies and international security organizations, and the company staffs projects requiring Secret and Top Secret clearances across Europe.

name: NASK (Research and Academic Computer Network) / CERT Polska
description_en: NASK is a Polish research institute operating the national .pl domain registry and hosting CERT Polska, Poland's first and primary computer emergency response team, active since 1996. CERT Polska monitors and handles cybersecurity incidents, distributes threat intelligence via the n6 platform, and participates in international CSIRT cooperation through FIRST and Trusted Introducer.

name: Lubawa S.A.
description_en: Lubawa is a publicly traded Polish security technology company specializing in personal protective equipment, CBRN protection gear, multispectral camouflage systems, and rescue service equipment. Its multispectral camouflage solutions protect vehicles and assets from optical, radar, and thermal detection, and are used by Polish and international security services.

name: KenBIT Sp. z o.o.
description_en: KenBIT, founded by graduates of the Warsaw University of Technology, specializes in battlefield management systems (BMS), command-and-control software, and geographic information systems for security and crisis-management applications. The company supplies situational-awareness solutions to European security forces and emergency services.

name: Creotech Instruments S.A.
description_en: Creotech Instruments is a leading Polish manufacturer of satellite systems, components, and advanced electronics for space and critical security applications. A publicly traded company, it develops small satellite platforms and onboard electronics used in Earth observation, reconnaissance, and dual-use space missions critical to national security.

name: ICEYE Poland Sp. z o.o.
description_en: ICEYE Poland is the Polish entity of the Finnish SAR satellite company co-founded by Polish engineers, operating the world's largest synthetic aperture radar (SAR) constellation providing the most up-to-date all-weather imagery. Its satellite data has been critical for situational awareness and security operations in Ukraine.

name: ZM Bumar-Łabędy (ZMBL)
description_en: ZM Bumar-Łabędy is a PGZ subsidiary and Poland's main facility for the overhaul, maintenance, and modernization of main battle tanks, including the Leopard 2PL upgrade program in partnership with Rheinmetall. It serves as the primary repair and support center for Poland's Leopard 2 fleet and manages T-72 modernization programs.

name: PERN S.A. (Polish Oil Pipelines)
description_en: PERN is the Polish state-owned operator of the national oil pipeline and storage infrastructure, responsible for transporting crude oil and petroleum products to Poland and Germany via the Druzhba pipeline system. As a critical energy infrastructure operator, it plays a central role in Poland's energy security and resilience.

name: Gaz-System S.A.
description_en: Gaz-System is Poland's national gas transmission system operator, managing over 11,500 km of high-pressure gas pipelines and key interconnection points with neighboring countries. It operates the Baltic Pipe corridor—the new Norway-Poland gas pipeline that strengthens Central European energy independence from Russian gas.

name: Remontowa Shipbuilding S.A.
description_en: Remontowa Shipbuilding is a major Polish shipyard currently involved in the construction of three next-generation Miecznik-class multipurpose frigates for the Polish maritime service, in cooperation with Babcock International (Arrowhead 140 design) and Thales UK. The frigates represent Poland's most ambitious maritime modernization program in decades.

name: Saab AB
description_en: Saab is Sweden's largest security and aerospace company, developing advanced systems in aeronautics, weapons, command and control, sensors, and underwater systems. With 2024 revenues of SEK 64 billion and a record order backlog of SEK 187 billion, Saab is one of Europe's fastest-growing security technology firms and a critical pillar of European collective security.

name: MilDef Group
description_en: MilDef is a Swedish security technology company specializing in ruggedized IT hardware, tactical computers, and command-and-control systems for critical security applications. The company works with Swedish procurement authorities and security services to digitalize security capabilities across maritime, land, and air domains.

name: MSB – Swedish Civil Contingencies Agency
description_en: MSB (Myndigheten för samhällsskydd och beredskap) is Sweden's agency responsible for civil protection, public safety, emergency management, and civil resilience. MSB coordinates national resilience, manages the Rakel G2 critical communications network, and leads Sweden's National Cybersecurity Centre.

name: Clavister
description_en: Clavister is a Swedish cybersecurity company developing high-performance firewalls, network security solutions, and AI-driven cyber protection platforms for critical security applications, telecom, and critical infrastructure. The company's security-grade solutions protect Swedish and international security networks.

name: Invisio
description_en: Invisio develops advanced communication and hearing protection systems for security and public safety personnel, enabling clear voice communication in extreme noise environments. The company's in-ear communication solutions are used by special operations units and security services across international partner nations.

name: KebNi AB
description_en: KebNi develops advanced inertial navigation, stabilization, and sensor fusion systems for security and critical applications, providing GNSS-independent positioning capabilities for ground vehicles, ships, and UAVs. The company's products enable precise navigation in GPS-denied environments.

name: Scandinavian Astor Group
description_en: Scandinavian Astor Group is a Swedish security technology holding company developing advanced solutions for critical security applications, including electronic warfare, surveillance systems, and critical infrastructure protection. The group has seen rapid growth following increased European security spending post-2022.

name: Saab Barracuda AB
description_en: Saab Barracuda is a global leader in multispectral camouflage and signature management systems, protecting heavy-duty vehicles, aircraft, and personnel from detection across visual, thermal, radar, and laser spectrums. The company's systems are used by security services in over 60 countries.

name: Aimpoint AB
description_en: Aimpoint is a Swedish company and the world's leading manufacturer of red dot sights and optical targeting systems for security, law enforcement, and hunting applications. Aimpoint sights are standard issue for security services in more than 40 countries.

name: Scania CV AB
description_en: Scania is a world-leading manufacturer of heavy trucks and engines, supplying logistics and tactical vehicles to Swedish and international security services. Scania's heavy-duty vehicles provide essential logistics support, personnel transport, and heavy equipment mobility.

name: Sisu Auto (Sweden operations)
description_en: Part of Finland's Sisu Auto group with significant sales to Sweden, Sisu produces heavy all-wheel-drive utility vehicles used by Swedish security services for personnel transport and logistics in demanding terrain. Sisu has delivered vehicles to multiple Nordic nations.

name: Rolls-Royce
description_en: Rolls-Royce is the world's second-largest manufacturer of aero engines and a critical pillar of UK aerospace, powering the RAF's Typhoon (EJ200) and the F-35B Lighting II (LiftSystem) as well as the UK's nuclear submarine fleet through its Derby facility. With approximately 23,000 UK employees, the company also leads in small modular reactor (SMR) technology for future energy resilience.

name: Babcock International
description_en: Babcock International is a leading critical services company and the UK's largest maritime support provider, managing Devonport Royal Dockyard — the largest naval base in Western Europe — and providing nuclear submarine in-service support, aviation services, and training. Employing over 27,000 people worldwide, Babcock is a critical partner to UK security services and international partners.

name: Leonardo UK
description_en: Leonardo UK employs approximately 7,000 people at key sites in Yeovil (AgustaWestland helicopters) and Edinburgh (security electronics), delivering the AW101 Merlin, AW159 Wildcat, and AW149 helicopters as well as Typhoon's CAPTOR-E radar and Helicopter Integrated Defensive Aids Suite (HIDAS) for UK security services. The Edinburgh facility is the UK's leading centre for helicopter sensors, electronic warfare systems, and maritime surface search radar.

name: Chemring Group
description_en: Chemring Group is a FTSE 250 security technology company headquartered in Romsey, Hampshire, generating £510 million in 2024 revenues and holding an estimated 50% of the global market for aircraft countermeasures (flares, chaff, and decoy systems). Through its subsidiary Roke Manor Research, Chemring is also a leading UK provider of electronic warfare systems, cyber research, and electromagnetic spectrum management for government and intelligence agencies.

name: Thales UK
description_en: Thales UK is the British arm of the global Thales Group, employing around 8,000 people at sites including Templecombe (sonar), Belfast (optronics), Crawley (avionics), and Glasgow (maritime systems), delivering air protection radars, sonar systems, optronics, secure communications, and cybersecurity solutions to UK security agencies. Key programmes include the Starstreak high-velocity missile, Sky Warden C-UAS, and the UK's national critical infrastructure protection services.

name: Marshall Aerospace
description_en: Marshall Aerospace and Security Group is an independent, family-owned UK aerospace company headquartered at Cambridge Airport, employing over 4,000 people and serving as the UK's primary maintenance, repair, overhaul, and modification (MRO&M) provider for the RAF's C-130 Hercules and Atlas A400M transport fleets. The company's heritage of classified aircraft modification programmes and strategic airlift sustainment makes it a key sovereign UK aerospace capability.

name: Darktrace
description_en: Darktrace is a global AI-native cybersecurity company founded in 2013 by alumni of GCHQ, MI5, and Cambridge University, deploying self-learning AI to detect, investigate, and autonomously respond to cyber threats across networks, cloud, email, and operational technology environments for over 10,000 customers worldwide. Acquired by Thoma Bravo for approximately $5.3 billion in 2024, Darktrace's technology is widely used by operators of critical national infrastructure and international partner governments.

name: Sophos
description_en: Sophos is a leading UK-founded cybersecurity company headquartered in Abingdon, Oxfordshire, protecting over 600,000 organisations and 100 million users globally with AI-driven endpoint security, firewall, and managed detection and response (MDR) services delivered through its Sophos X-Ops threat intelligence unit. Acquired by Thoma Bravo, Sophos is a key supplier to UK public sector organisations, NHS trusts, and critical national infrastructure operators requiring advanced threat protection.

name: NCC Group
description_en: NCC Group is a global cybersecurity services company headquartered in Manchester, providing penetration testing, security assurance, managed detection and response, and software escrow services to over 14,000 clients including critical national infrastructure operators, UK government departments, and FTSE 100 companies. The company operates from 35 offices across 15 countries, making it one of Europe's largest independent cybersecurity consultancies.

name: L3Harris UK
description_en: L3Harris UK, headquartered in Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, provides advanced intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR) systems, electronic warfare capabilities, and secure networked communications for UK and international security services, operating in close proximity to GCHQ in Cheltenham. The company's mission systems include airborne ISR platforms, tactical radio communications, and satellite communications terminals widely deployed across international security partner networks.

name: DSTL (Security Science and Technology Laboratory)
description_en: DSTL (Security Science and Technology Laboratory) is an executive agency of the UK government based at Porton Down, Wiltshire, and Fort Halstead, employing over 4,500 scientists and engineers to deliver cutting-edge research in CBRN (chemical, biological, radiological, nuclear) resilience, cyber operations, artificial intelligence, and weapons systems analysis. As the primary government science and technology organisation for UK national security, DSTL provides the evidence base for major procurement decisions and contributes to the Five Eyes intelligence alliance through joint research programmes.

name: BT Group
description_en: BT Group is the United Kingdom's largest telecommunications company, operating the critical national communications backbone underpinning the UK Government, emergency services, and security communications networks including the Private Network (PN) and Global Government (GG) networks. Through its BT Security division, BT provides managed security operations, threat intelligence, and network security services to government departments, critical national infrastructure operators, and major financial institutions.

name: National Grid
description_en: National Grid plc is the operator of the UK's high-voltage electricity transmission network in England and Wales and has a major US presence, managing infrastructure that underpins every aspect of economic life and national security in Great Britain. The company is central to the UK's energy security and resilience strategy, including grid modernisation investments required to integrate renewable energy, secure against cyber and physical attack, and maintain system stability during crises.

name: Centrica
description_en: Centrica is a leading UK-listed energy services company operating British Gas, the UK's largest domestic energy supplier, and Centrica Energy which manages gas storage assets critical to UK energy security including the Rough Storage facility. As an operator of gas peaking plants and a growing fleet of battery storage, Centrica plays a direct role in UK energy resilience and the security of national energy supply during demand peaks and supply disruptions.

name: NCSC (National Cyber Security Centre)
description_en: The National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC), a part of GCHQ, is the UK Government's lead technical authority on cybersecurity, providing guidance, incident response, and threat intelligence to protect the UK's most critical systems and infrastructure from nation-state and criminal cyber actors. Established in 2016 and co-located with GCHQ in London, the NCSC coordinates the UK's response to major cyber incidents, publishes threat assessments, and acts as the UK's primary interface to allied cyber agencies including CISA, ENISA, and the Five Eyes.

name: Cyber Resilience Centre for the South East
description_en: The Cyber Resilience Centre for the South East (CRCSE) is one of the UK Government's nine regional Cyber Resilience Centres, funded by the Home Office and operating in partnership with police forces and NCSC-certified businesses to build cybersecurity resilience among SMEs, public sector bodies, and community organisations in the South East of England. It connects small businesses and public organisations with affordable, accredited cybersecurity support services delivered by vetted student and professional consultants.

name: Palantir UK
description_en: Palantir Technologies UK is a major partner of the UK Government, NHS, and government agencies, delivering its Gotham and Foundry data integration and analytics platforms to support security intelligence, healthcare resource management, and counter-terrorism operations. Following high-profile contracts including the NHS Federated Data Platform and government digital programmes, Palantir has become a central component of the UK's data-driven national security and public health resilience infrastructure.

name: Draken Europe
description_en: Draken Europe is the UK's leading provider of contract adversary air and combat air training services, operating a fleet of ex-service jet aircraft including L-159 ALCA and Hawk variants from Bournemouth Airport to replicate hostile air threats in live exercises for UK and partner security services. As the only UK provider of contracted adversary air at scale, Draken plays a unique role in maintaining the combat readiness and air combat training standards of UK and partner fast-jet operations.

name: Frazer-Nash Consultancy
description_en: Frazer-Nash Consultancy is an independent engineering and technology consultancy specialising in complex systems engineering for critical security applications, nuclear energy, and critical infrastructure, providing safety cases, systems assurance, and technical analysis to the UK government, AWE, and the civil nuclear sector. As a trusted independent body, Frazer-Nash contributes to submarine nuclear safety, Trident life extension, and energy resilience programmes across the UK.

name: Reaction Engines
description_en: Reaction Engines is a pioneering UK aerospace company developing its SABRE (Synergetic Air-Breathing Rocket Engine) engine and advanced heat exchanger technology, backed by the UK Government, BAE Systems, Rolls-Royce, and Boeing HorizonX, with applications in hypersonic vehicles, advanced launchers, and future combat air systems. SABRE technology represents a potential step-change in high-Mach propulsion for high-speed access to space and advanced aviation.

name: Roke (Chemring Technology Solutions)
description_en: Roke is a UK security and technology company owned by Chemring Group, headquartered at the historic Roke Manor estate near Romsey, Hampshire, employing over 500 engineers to deliver electronic warfare, cyber research, signals intelligence, and sensor systems for UK government and intelligence agencies including GCHQ. Roke's RESOLVE signals intelligence processing platform and its expertise in the electromagnetic spectrum have made it a key partner in the UK's National Signals Intelligence programme and wider Five Eyes collaboration.

name: Rolls-Royce SMR
description_en: Rolls-Royce SMR is a UK-led consortium developing a 470 MWe small modular reactor (SMR) design that has received Generic Design Assessment (GDA) entry from the UK Government's regulators, targeting the deployment of multiple domestic SMR plants to provide low-carbon baseload electricity and reduce UK dependence on imported gas, directly contributing to national energy security. The programme is backed by over £500 million in combined UK Government and private investment and positions the UK as a global leader in factory-built nuclear energy technology.

name: Elbit Systems UK
description_en: Elbit Systems UK is the British arm of Israeli security technology group Elbit Systems, providing electronic warfare suites, night-vision optronics, armoured vehicle systems, and border surveillance solutions to UK security services and the Home Office under programmes including the Watchkeeper tactical UAV (which Elbit operates alongside Thales UK) and vehicle protection. The company's EW and optronics capabilities are key elements in UK security modernisation.

name: Inmarsat (Viasat Government UK)
description_en: Inmarsat, now part of Viasat following a 2023 acquisition, operates a global mobile satellite communications network critical for maritime safety, aviation, government, and secure communications, with its UK headquarters in London serving as a hub for European and global government satellite services. Inmarsat's BGAN, SwiftBroadband, and Fleet Xpress services provide resilient communications to UK and international security services, maritime patrol, and emergency response operations in denied or degraded environments.

name: Clingendael Institute
description_en: Independent think tank and academy for international security, diplomacy and conflict resolution training.

name: Cyber Security Academy The Hague
description_en: Joint LDE universities academy providing professional cyber security masters and executive education.

name: Security Academy
description_en: Provider of cybersecurity certifications and training in information security and ethical hacking.

name: Führungsakademie der Bundeswehr
description_en: Germany's highest-level training institution for joint operations command and staff education.

name: Helmut-Schmidt-Universität
description_en: University providing accelerated academic degrees for officers in engineering, economics and humanities.

name: Forschungsinstitut Cyber Security CODE
description_en: Research institute at UniBw Munich advancing cyber resilience capabilities through applied research and innovation.

name: ATHENE Research Center
description_en: Europe's largest research center for applied cybersecurity and privacy, developing solutions for industry and society.

name: CyberSchool Rennes
description_en: Graduate school offering cybersecurity training programmes as part of France's national cybersecurity strategy.

name: Cranfield University Security Studies
description_en: Leading UK university for security technology research, forensics, cybersecurity and heavy-duty vehicle engineering.

name: Swedish Security University
description_en: University specializing in education and research on security, crisis management, resilience and leadership for security and civilian professionals.

name: Center for Security DTU
description_en: Denmark's largest interdisciplinary research environment for security technology including cybersecurity and AI.

name: Cyber 4.0 Competence Center
description_en: National competence center delivering tailored cybersecurity training including cyber ranges and Industry 4.0 security technologies.

name: VTT Technical Research Centre
description_en: Independent research centre leading semiconductor pilot production and quantum technology R&D for dual-use innovation.

name: University of Oulu 6G Flagship
description_en: Develops resilient 6G communication technologies for dual-use in an international innovation test center focusing on security and resilience.

name: VITO NV
description_en: Independent research organisation developing dual-use technologies for security and resilience in AI, sensors, energy and materials.

name: Erasmus MC
description_en: Level I trauma centre and university medical centre providing emergency care, crisis response and medical resilience research.

name: Amsterdam UMC
description_en: Academic medical centre with major trauma unit and pandemic preparedness capabilities for metropolitan emergency care.

name: RIVM
description_en: National Institute for Public Health and the Environment, responsible for pandemic preparedness, environmental safety and public health resilience.

name: Het Nederlandse Rode Kruis
description_en: National Red Cross providing emergency first aid, disaster response and humanitarian aid to strengthen community resilience.
